examples.title: Node Structure Definition in C
examples.description: Defines a self-referential structure for singly linked list node
examples.explanation: The self-referential structure contains data and a pointer to the next node. malloc() allocates memory dynamically on heap. Each node is independent and connected via pointers.

examples.title: Singly Linked List Implementation in Python
examples.description: Complete implementation with insert and delete operations
examples.explanation: Python class-based implementation. insert_at_beginning: O(1), delete_from_beginning: O(1), traverse: O(n). Uses None instead of NULL.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for insert/delete at beginning, O(n) for traverse

examples.title: Linked Stack Operations
examples.description: Stack implementation using linked list with push and pop
examples.explanation: Linked stack uses head as top. Push adds at beginning (O(1)), Pop removes from beginning (O(1)). No overflow unless heap memory exhausted. Uses double pointer to modify head.

examples.title: Linked Queue Operations
examples.description: Queue implementation using linked list with enqueue and dequeue
examples.explanation: Uses two pointers: front for deletion, rear for insertion. Enqueue: O(1), Dequeue: O(1). When front becomes NULL, rear also set to NULL. Both operations constant time.

examples.title: Polynomial Representation using Linked List
examples.description: Represents polynomial as linked list with terms sorted by exponent
examples.explanation: Each term (coefficient, exponent) stored as node. Nodes sorted by exponent (descending). Addition merges two polynomials by comparing exponents. Efficient for sparse polynomials.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m + n) where m and n are number of terms
